zh.Gilmerton, Midlothian, Scotland: 蘇格蘭米德洛迪安吉爾莫頓
zh.lifeSummary: Sir John Herring of Edmonstone - Lord of Gilmerton, Drum and Goodtrees於1240年出生在蘇格蘭米德洛迪安吉爾莫頓。他和Clarissy Nye至少有1個兒子和1個女兒。他埋葬在蘇格蘭艾丁波。

zh-hans.Gilmerton, Midlothian, Scotland: 苏格兰米德洛迪安吉爾莫頓
zh-hans.lifeSummary: Sir John Herring of Edmonstone - Lord of Gilmerton, Drum and Goodtrees于1240年出生在苏格兰米德洛迪安吉爾莫頓。他和Clarissy Nye至少有1个儿子和1个女儿。他埋葬在苏格兰艾丁波。

nameMeanings.name: Herring
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (London), Dutch, and German: metonymic occupational name for a herring fisher or for a seller of the fish, or perhaps for someone who habitually ate herring, from Middle English hering (Old English hǣring, hēring), Dutch haring, Middle High German hærinc. In some cases it may have been a nickname in the sense of a trifle, something of little value, a meaning which is found in medieval phrases and proverbial expressions such as ‘to like neither herring nor barrel’, i.e. not to like something at all.
German: habitational name from Herringen in Westphalia.
German and Jewish (Ashkenazic): variant of   Hering  .

variants.name: Hearing
meanings: English (Yorkshire and Lancashire): variant of   Herring  .
Americanized form of German Häring (see Haring ) or of some other similar (like-sounding) surname.

variants.name: Herrin
meanings: English: variant of   Herring  .
French: rare variant of   Herin  .

variants.name: Herrig
meanings: German: habitational name from any of several places called Herrig in the Rhineland or from Herringen in Westphalia. See also   Herring  .

variants.name: Hering
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Hasso, Alfons, Alois, Arno, Ernst, Erwin, Florian, Gerhard, Guenther, Kurt, Ludwig, Manfred.

German and Jewish (Ashkenazic): from Middle High German hærinc ‘herring’, German Hering, a metonymic occupational name for a fisherman, particularly a seller of herrings, or a nickname for someone supposedly resembling a herring. In some cases the Jewish surname is artificial. In North America, this surname is also an altered form of the German and Jewish variant Häring. Compare Haring .

German and Dutch: habitational name from any of several places and farmhouses so named, for instance near Zeesse in Overijssel, originally inhabited by a certain Here or Hero and his kin. Here or Hero is a short form of an ancient Germanic personal name with the element heri ‘army’.

nameMeanings.name: of
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese: Cheng, Jing, Kam Fung, Wai Shing, Wing, Yali, Yuk.
Chinese:
variant Romanization of the surnames 歐 and 區, see   Ou   1 and 2.

variants.name: Ou
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Wei, Ying, Ching, Heng, Hong, Jia, Kuang Yu, Li-Ching, Cho, Chong, Chung, Sa.  Southeast Asian Lichun, Ming, Quan, Roeun, Sokhom, Chau, Hai, Leang, Ly, Nhanh, Phan, Pho, Tha.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 歐, meaning ‘to sing someone's praise’ in ancient Chinese: (i) from the placename Dong Ou (東甌), a fief (in present-day Wenzhou in Zhejiang province) which was granted to Yao in 192  BC . It is said that Yao was a descendant of Wujiang (reigned 355–333  BC ), king of the state of Yue (located mainly in present-day Zhejiang and Jiangxi provinces) during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ). (ii) from the placename Ou Yu (歐餘), the name of a fief (located in present-day Zhejiang province) that was granted to Ti, the second son of Wujiang (reigned 355–333  BC ), king of the state of Yue (located mainly in present-day Zhejiang and Jiangxi provinces) during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ).

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 區, originally denoting an ancient vessel for measurement in ancient Chinese: from the personal name of Ou Zhizi (歐治子), an expert sword-maker who lived during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476 BC ). Some of his descendants inherited Ou (歐) as their surname, simplifying the Chinese character 歐 to 區.

nameMeanings.name: Edmonstone
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of Family Names in Britain and Ireland © University of the West of England 2016
meanings: Scottish:  see  Edmonston  .

variants.name: Edmonston
meanings: Scottish: variant of   Edmiston  .

nameMeanings.name: Lord
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: nickname from Middle English laford, louerd, lord ‘lord, master’ (Old English hlāford), perhaps given to a someone who played the part of the lord or master of ceremonies in festival games.
Irish: adoption of the English name (see 1 above) as a translation of the main element of Gaelic Ó Tighearnaigh (see Tierney ) and Mac Thighearnáin (see McKiernan ).
Americanized form of French Laure, a variant of the personal name Laur 3.

variants.name: Tierney
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Irish Brendan, Eamon, John Patrick, Brigid, Clodagh, Kieran, Michael Sean, Siobhan.

Irish: shortened form of O'Tierney, an Anglicized form of Gaelic Ó Tíghearnaigh ‘descendant of Tighearnach’, a byname meaning ‘lord, master’. The Gaelic name is sometimes ‘translated’ as English Lord . Compare Tiernan .

variants.name: Lords
meanings: English: variant of Lord , with post-medieval excrescent -s. This surname is rare in Britain.

nameMeanings.name: of
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese: Cheng, Jing, Kam Fung, Wai Shing, Wing, Yali, Yuk.
Chinese:
variant Romanization of the surnames 歐 and 區, see   Ou   1 and 2.

variants.name: Ou
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Wei, Ying, Ching, Heng, Hong, Jia, Kuang Yu, Li-Ching, Cho, Chong, Chung, Sa.  Southeast Asian Lichun, Ming, Quan, Roeun, Sokhom, Chau, Hai, Leang, Ly, Nhanh, Phan, Pho, Tha.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 歐, meaning ‘to sing someone's praise’ in ancient Chinese: (i) from the placename Dong Ou (東甌), a fief (in present-day Wenzhou in Zhejiang province) which was granted to Yao in 192  BC . It is said that Yao was a descendant of Wujiang (reigned 355–333  BC ), king of the state of Yue (located mainly in present-day Zhejiang and Jiangxi provinces) during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ). (ii) from the placename Ou Yu (歐餘), the name of a fief (located in present-day Zhejiang province) that was granted to Ti, the second son of Wujiang (reigned 355–333  BC ), king of the state of Yue (located mainly in present-day Zhejiang and Jiangxi provinces) during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ).

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 區, originally denoting an ancient vessel for measurement in ancient Chinese: from the personal name of Ou Zhizi (歐治子), an expert sword-maker who lived during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476 BC ). Some of his descendants inherited Ou (歐) as their surname, simplifying the Chinese character 歐 to 區.

nameMeanings.name: Gilmerton,
meanings: Anglo-Saxon: Wilmer, Wilmer, and tun, house, meaning home of Wilmer

nameMeanings.name: Drum
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Irish (Fermanagh and Cavan): shortened Anglicized form of Gaelic Ó Droma ‘descendant of Droma’, a personal name, which may be from druim ‘back, ridge’ and have been given originally to someone with a deformed back. Compare Drumm .
German: nickname from Middle Low German drum, Middle High German trum ‘woodblock, uncouth person’.

variants.name: Drumm
meanings: Irish (Cavan, Fermanagh, and Meath): variant of   Drum   1.
Scottish: habitational name from a place and castle in Aberdeenshire named from Gaelic druim ‘ridge’.
German: from Middle Low German drum ‘lump of wood’ (Middle High German trum), used as a nickname for an uncouth man.

variants.name: Drummey
meanings: Irish: variant of   Drum  .

nameMeanings.name: and
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Arabic/Muslim Ali, Hossein, Abbas, Behnaz, Gitti, Hamid, Jafar, Mansour, Masoud, Mehdi, Pari, Rana.  Jewish Jechiel, Bracha, Herschel, Morry.
Iranian: from Zand, the name of a dinasty which ruled Persia 1751–94. It is based on Middle Persian zand ‘great, powerful’ and was originally the name of a former Iranian tribe.
Jewish (Ashkenazic): variant of   Sand  .

variants.name: Sand
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Scandinavian Erik, Bertel, Ejnar, Lars, Levinus, Nels, Niels, Thor.

English, Danish, Norwegian, Swedish, German, and Jewish (Ashkenazic): topographic name for someone who lived on or near a patch of sandy soil, from the vocabulary word sand (German Sand). As a Swedish name it was often purely ornamental, and as a Jewish artificial. It is also found in France (see 3 below).
English: habitational name from a place such as Send (Surrey) or Sound (Cheshire), named with Middle English sand, sond ‘sand’.

variants.name: Zant
meanings: German: variant of   Zand  .
Dutch: variant of   Van Zandt  .

variants.name: Zandi
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Arabic/Muslim Mahmud, Taher, Ahmad, Azi, Bahram, Behzad, Farman, Firooz, Hamid, Hossein, Jaleh, Masood.
Iranian: derivative of   Zand  , denoting descent from or association with a Persian dinasty of that name.
Italian (Emilia): perhaps a nickname from the dialect word gianda ‘acorn’.

nameMeanings.name: John
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: New Testament: ‘God is gracious’. See John .

en.lifeSummary: Sir John Herring of Edmonstone - Lord of Gilmerton, Drum and Goodtrees was born in 1240, in Gilmerton, Midlothian, Scotland. He had at least 1 son and 1 daughter with Clarissy Nye. He was buried in Edinburgh, Scotland.

fr.lifeSummary: Sir John Herring of Edmonstone - Lord of Gilmerton, Drum and Goodtrees est né en 1240, à Gilmerton, Midlothian, Écosse. Il a eu au moins 1 fils et 1 fille avec Clarissy Nye. Ilenterré à Edinburgh, Écosse.

de.lifeSummary: Sir John Herring of Edmonstone - Lord of Gilmerton, Drum and Goodtrees wurde 1240 in Gilmerton, Midlothian, Scotland geboren. Er hatte mit Clarissy Nye mindestens 1 Sohn und 1 Tochter. Er wurde in Edinburgh, Scotland begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Sir John Herring of Edmonstone - Lord of Gilmerton, Drum and Goodtrees è nato il nel 1240 nel Gilmerton, Midlothian, Scotland. Lui avuto almeno 1 figlio e 1 figlia con Clarissy Nye. Fu sepolto a Edinburgh, Scotland.

ja.lifeSummary: Sir John Herring of Edmonstone - Lord of Gilmerton, Drum and Goodtreesは1240年にスコットランドミドロスアンギルマートンで生まれた。彼はClarissy Nyeとの間に少なくとも1人の息子と1人の娘をもうけた。彼はスコットランドエディンブルグにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Sir John Herring of Edmonstone - Lord of Gilmerton, Drum and Goodtrees(은)는 1240년 스컷랜드 미들로스이언 길머턴에서 태어났다. 그는 Clarissy Nye(와)과 적어도 한 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 스컷랜드 에딘버그h에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Sir John Herring of Edmonstone - Lord of Gilmerton, Drum and Goodtrees nasceu em 1240, em Gilmerton, Midlothian, Scotland. Ele teve pelo menos 1 filho e 1 filha com Clarissy Nye. Ele foi sepultado em Edinburgh, Scotland.

ru.lifeSummary: Sir John Herring of Edmonstone - Lord of Gilmerton, Drum and Goodtrees родился в 1240 г. в местности джилмиртон, мидлотян, Шотландия. У него был по крайней мере 1 сын и 1 дочь от Clarissy Nye. Он был похоронен в местности эдинбург, Шотландия.

es.lifeSummary: Sir John Herring of Edmonstone - Lord of Gilmerton, Drum and Goodtrees nació en 1240, en Gilmerton, Midlothian, Scotland. Tuvo por lo menos 1 hijo y 1 hija con Clarissy Nye. Fue sepultado en Edinburgh, Scotland.
